analogy: Think of "Local Governments" through the chapter's own opening images: 176 Indian Constitution at Work Chapter Eight LOCAL GOVERNMENTS INTRODUCTION In a democracy, it is not sufficient to have an elected government at the centre and at the State level. It is also necessary that even at the local level, there should be an elected... The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: LOCAL, GOVERNMENTS, INTRODUCTION, WHY LOCAL GOVERNMENTS?. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Local Governments is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that 176 Indian Constitution at Work Chapter Eight LOCAL GOVERNMENTS INTRODUCTION In a democracy, it is not sufficient to have an elected government at the centre and at the State level.

The text explains that it is also necessary that even at the local level, there should be an elected government to look after local affairs.

The text explains that in this chapter, you will study the structure of local government in our country.

The text explains that you will also study the importance of the local governments and ways to give them independent powers.

The text explains that after studying this chapter, you will know: ± the importance of local government bodies; ± the provisions made by the 73r d and 74t h amendments; and ± functions and responsibilities of the local government bodies.

The text explains that 177 Chapter 8: Local Governments WHY LOCAL GOVERNMENTS?

The text explains that geeta Rathore belongs to Jamonia Talab Gram Panchayat, Sehore district, Madhya Pradesh.

The text explains that she was elected Sarpanch in 1995 from a reserved seat; but in 2000, the village people rewarded her for her admirable work by electing her again - this time from a non-reserved seat.

The text explains that from a housewife, Geeta has grown into a leader displaying political farsightedness - she has harnessed the collective energy of her Panchayat to renovate water tanks, build a school building, construct village roads, fight against domestic violence and atrocities against women, create environmental awareness, and encourage afforestation and water management in her village. —Panchayati Raj Update, Vol.

The text explains that there is another story of yet another woman achiever.

The text explains that she was the President (Sarpanch) of a Gram Panchayat of Vengaivasal village in Tamil Nadu.

The text explains that in 1997, the Tamil Nadu government allotted two hectares of land to 71 government employees.

The text explains that this piece of land fell within the vicinity of this Gram Panchayat.

The text explains that on the instructions of higher authorities the District Collector of Kancheepuram directed the President of the Gram Panchayat to pass a resolution endorsing the allotment of the said land for the purpose already decided.

The text explains that the President and the Gram Panchayat refused to pass such an order and the Collector issued an order to acquire the land.

The text explains that the Gram Panchayat filed a writ petition in the Madras High Court against the Collector’s action.

The text explains that the judges held that the government order amounted to not only infringement of the powers of the Panchayats but a gross violation of the constitutional status of the Panchayats. — Panchayati Raj Update, Vol.

The text explains that they are representative of a larger transformation that is taking place across India especially after constitutional status was accorded to local government institutions in 1993.

The text explains that 178 Indian Constitution at Work Local government is government at the village and district level.

The text explains that local government is about government closest to the common people.

The text explains that local government is about government that involves the day-to-day life and problems of ordinary citizens.

The text explains that local government believes that local knowledge and local interest are essential ingredients for democratic decision making.

The text explains that the advantage of local government is that it is so near the people.

The text explains that it is convenient for the people to approach the local government for solving their problems both quickly and with minimum cost.

The text explains that vengaivasal village is able to still retain its land and the right to decide what to do with it because of the relentless efforts of its Gram Panchayat President and members.

The text explains that so, local governments can be very effective in protecting the local interests of the people.

The text explains that strong and vibrant local governments ensure both active participation and purposeful accountability.

The text explains that vengaivasal village Gram Panchayat’s relentless efforts to secure its rights over its own land were an example of a mission to ensure accountability.

The text explains that it is at the level of local government that common citizens can be involved in decision making concerning their lives, their needs and above all their development.

The text explains that common people are more familiar with their local government than with the government at the State or national level.

The text explains that they are also more concerned with what local government does or has failed to do as it has a direct bearing and impact on Is it possible that we only had governments at the local level and a coordinating body at the national level?

The text explains that 179 Chapter 8: Local Governments their day-to-day life.

The text explains that thus, strengthening local government is like strengthening democratic processes.

The text explains that check your progress ± How does local government strengthen democracy? ± In the example given above, what do you think the Government of Tamil Nadu should have done?

The text explains that gROWTH OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT IN INDIA Let us now discuss how local government has grown in India and what our Constitution says about it.

The text explains that in modern times, elected local government bodies were created after 1882.

The text explains that however, due to slow progress in this regard, the Indian National Congress urged the government to take necessary steps to make all local bodies more effective.

The text explains that following the Government of India Act 1919, village panchayats were established in a number of provinces.

The text explains that this trend continued after the Government of India Act of 1935.

The text explains that 180 Indian Constitution at Work order to be successful.

The text explains that in this structure composed of innumerable villages, there will be ever- widening, ever-ascending circles.

The text explains that life will be a pyramid with the apex sustained by the bottom - Mahatma Gandhi When the Constitution was prepared, the subject of local government was assigned to the States.

The text explains that it was also mentioned in the Directive Principles as one of the policy directives to all governments in the country.

The text explains that as you have read in Chapter 2, being a part of the Directive Principles of State Policy, this provision of the Constitution was non-justiciable and primarily advisory in its nature.

The text explains that how would you decide the suitable day for the meeting?

The text explains that think who would be able to attend / not attend the meeting because of your choice.

The text explains that (i) A day specified by the BDO or the collector (ii) Day of the village haat (iii) Sunday (iv) Naag panchami / sankranti b.

The text explains that (i) Venue suggested by the circular of the district collector.

The text explains that in the Gram Sabha meeting firstly a circular sent by the district collector was read.

The text explains that it suggested what steps should be taken to organise an education rally and what should be its route.

The text explains that the meeting did not discuss about the children who never come to school or about girls’ education, or the condition of the school building and the timing of the school.

The text explains that no women teacher attended the meeting as it was held on Sunday.

Source: NCERT Class 11 Indian Constitution at Work, Chapter 8: Local Governments

prelims_traps: Do not treat LOCAL and GOVERNMENTS as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Local Governments; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Local Governments through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use LOCAL and GOVERNMENTS to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
